label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this question. So, the story is about Mr. Williams who was driving and encountered a sign that said "SLOW". He slowed down, looked both ways, and then drove across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams explained that the sign wasn't a "STOP" sign but a "SLOW" one, and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly First, let's recap the key points from the story. The sign said "SLOW", so drivers need to reduce speed but don't have to stop unless there's traffic. Mr. Williams slowed down, checked both directions, and then drove across without a full stop. The policeman accused him of not stopping, but Mr. Williams pointed out the sign. The policeman then realized his mistake and left. Now, analyzing the options. The question is about what Mr. Williams was doing at the moment he was stopped. Let's check each option against the story. Option A: was driving at a high speed. But the story says he slowed down. So this is probably incorrect because he was not going fast, he was slowing. Option B: was driving onto the main road. The story mentions that he came to a crossroad where the sign said "SLOW". The main road is ahead. He slowed down when he came to the main road. So he was on the main road? Wait, wait. Wait, the sign says "SLOW" when approaching the main road. Wait, the context says that at some crossroads, the sign says to stop when coming to the main road, others
